3723.history: HMAS Lismore served at the British East Indies Station from June 1941 onwards. The ship was based in Colombo and later in the Red Sea. In May 1943 she went to the Mediterranean and was involved in the Sicily landings the following July. HMAS Lismore went back to the Eastern Fleet in October 1943 and was transferred to the British Pacific Fleet in 1945. Decommissioned and transferred to the Royal Netherlands Navy on 3 July 1946 being renamed Hr.Ms. Batjan.

3751.history: Around 0345hours on 15 November 1942 HMS Algerine (Lt.Cdr. Wilfrid Alan Cooke, RN) was torpedoed and sunk off Bougie, Algeria in position 36º45'N, 05º11'E by the Italian submarine Ascianghi. The minesweeper was taking part in Operation Torch and had been tasked with clearing Bougie Roads from Axis mines, mission which was accomplished succesfully with the destruction of 46 mines. Ascianghi (Lt. Rino Ereler) had sailed from Trapani on the 9th for the Bougie area: at 0339 hrs the boat, which had moved to an area closer inshore, spotted a formation of 3 warships, initially identified as a cruiser escorted by 2 destroyers, and closed to attack. The submarine fired 2 torpedoes aimed at the second vessel in the line and missed, then turned and at 0346 hrs fired a second 2-torpedo salvo, this time against the tail unit. Lt Ereler believed both torpedoes had hit, forward of the bridge and and between bridge and stack. The submarine disengaged without suffering any reaction and reached Napoli safely on the 18th. The ship attacked was HMS Algerine, which sank about 5 nautical miles north of Cap Carbon. 32 survivors were rescued by HMS Cadmus and taken to Bougie. Of these 32 survivors 24 later died due to internal wounds caused by the the detonation of the minesweepers own depth charges, which detonated at their preset depth. The remaining 8 survivors were on a carley reft and thus were not wounded when the depth charges exploded.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Wilfrid Alan Cooke, RN (11 Nov 1941 - 15 Nov 1942 (+))

commanders: T/Cdr. George Edward Walter Wentworth Bayly, RNVR (8 Dec 1943 - 9 Nov 1944)
A/Cdr. Andrew Edward Doran, DSC, RN (9 Nov 1944 - )

3775.history: Rifleman spent some years during the early 1960's in the Vickers Shipyard at Barrow-in-Furness as an accomodation ship for Royal Navy crews attending the building of RN submarines especially the two Polaris Submarines Resolution and Repulse.
commanders: Lt. Charles Laurence Carroll, DSC, RNR (16 Jan 1944 - Nov 1945)
Cdr. David Lampen, DSO, RN (1 May 1943 - 30 Aug 1943)
Cdr. Colin Henry Corbet-Singleton, DSC, RN (30 Aug 1943 - Feb 1945)
Cdr. Frederick Stanley Walford, RN (Feb 1945 - mid 1946)

3777.history: Sold to the Belgian Navy on 15 January 1953 being renamed P. De Moor (F 905). Scrapped 1968/69.
commanders: A/Cdr. Frank Eric Brooking, RN (Jun 1943 - Jan 1945)
A/Cdr. Robert William Wainewright, RN (Jan 1945 - early 1946)

3778.history: Decommissioned at South Queensferry, Scotland in December 1947. The ship was placed in reserve. Sold to the Belgian Navy on 25 February 1953.
commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. William Harold Brittain, DSC, RNR (Jun 1943 - 24 Nov 1943)
Lt.Cdr. Leonard Bentley Moffatt, RNR (24 Nov 1943 - Oct 1944)
Lt. Gerald Sydney Willis, RNR (Oct 1944 - 16 Mar 1946)

3779.history: HMS Squirrel (Lt.Cdr. Malcolm Buist, RN) was mined on 24 July 1945 off Phuket, Siam (Thailand). 7 of the crew died.
commanders: Lt. Malcolm Buist, RN (Jun 1944 - 24 Jul 1945)

3780.history: HMS Vestal (Lt.Cdr. Charles William Porter, DSC, RN) was sunk by a Japanese kamikaze aircraft about 55 nautical miles south-west of Phuket, Siam (Thailand) in position 07º05'N, 97º50'E.
commanders: Lt. Charles William Porter, DSC, RN (26 Jul 1943 - 26 Jul 1945)

3781.history: Algerine class minesweeper commissioned in 1945. She was nominated for reduction in Jan 1948 to Reserve status at Harwich. She was laid up at Harwich until 1951 when she was once again nominated for service in 4th Minesweeping Flotilla as Senior Officer's ship (MS4) on formation. This was part of a programme to increase Royal Navy capabilities following the outbreak of war in Korea. Scrapped in August 1961.
commanders: Cdr. Robert Thory Gardiner, RN (6 Jul 1945 - 6 Jan 1947)
Lt.Cdr. Walter James Taylor, RN (1948 - 1950)

3782.history: HMS Cockatrice was adopted by the Parish of Yelvertoft in Northamptonshire during Warship Week. Scrapped on 29 August 1963.
commanders: A/Lt.Cdr. Cyril William Armstrong, RNR (2 Mar 1943 - 11 Jul 1945)
T/Lt. James Douglas Stewart, RNR (11 Jul 1945 - 19 Nov 1945)
A/Lt.Cdr. Harry Sobey, RNR (7 May 1945 - 6 Oct 1945)

3786.history: Sold to the Royal Persian Navy on 30 July 1949.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Robert Bruce Chandler, RN (7 Sep 1942 - 5 Jul 1943)
Capt. John Wilfrid Boutwood, RN (5 Jul 1943 - Oct 1943)
A/Cdr. Geoffrey Noel Rawlings, DSO, RN (Oct 1943 - Oct 1945)
A/Cdr. (retired) Arthur Hugh Wynne-Edwards, RN (1 Nov 1942 - Dec 1944)
Cdr. Andrew Douglas Holden, OBE, RNZNR (Dec 1944 - 24 Sep 1945)
T/A/Cdr. Frederick Bradley, DSC, RNVR (24 Sep 1945 - late 1945)

3790.history: HMS Hydra (T/A/Lt.Cdr. Charles Thomas Jegon Wellard, RNR) was mined off Ostend, Belgium on 10 November 1944. Declared constructive total loss. Scrapped in 1947.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Harry Whittaker Brammall, DSO, DSC, RNR (11 Jan 1943 - 27 Oct 1943)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Charles Thomas Jegon Wellard, RNR (27 Oct 1943 - late 1944)

3792.history: Allocated to 7th Minesweeper Flotilla. 31st July 1944, sailed for Mediterranean. Allied landings south coast of France 15th August 1944.(Operation Dragoon) Beached at Poros, East Coast of Greece, 16th October 1944 after being badly damaged by a mine the previous day during Operation Edgehill. Out of action for the rest of WWII. Sold to Italy in 1946.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. James Ernest Clark, DSC, RN (4 Nov 1943 - 3 Jul 1944)
Cdr. Humphrey Leoline Jenkins, RN (3 Jul 1944 - Oct 1944)
Lt.Cdr. Thomas Tod Euman, RN (20 Dec 1943 - 12 Feb 1945)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Charles Henry Walton, RNR (12 Feb 1945 - 10 Nov 1945)

3795.history: Used as a target ship for Seacat missiles fired from the Seacat trials ship, HMS Decoy, in Lyme Bay, resulting in Orestes sinking.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. Alwyn William Reginald Adams, RN (22 Mar 1943 - 16 Oct 1944)
A/Lt.Cdr. Derek Harold George Coughlan, DSC, RNR (16 Oct 1944 - 4 Oct 1946)

3796.history: Sold to the South African Navy in 1947 being renamed SAS Pietermaritzburg. Scuttled to become an artificial reef off Simonstown on 12 November 1994.

3805.history: Transferred to the Royal Navy upon completion. Returned to the United States Navy in January 1947.

Lt.Cdr. Edward James Prevost, RN (27 Sep 1944 - early 1945)

3806.history: Returned to the United States Navy in 1946. Became the Greek Pyrpolitis in 1947.
commanders: T/Lt. Harry Reginald Grief, RNR (23 Oct 1943 - Nov 1945)

3807.history: Returned to the United States Navy in 1946. Became the Greek Armatolos in 1947.
commanders: Cdr. William Butler Tanner, RD, RNR (27 Apr 1943 - 1 Nov 1943)

3811.history: Returned to United States Navy in January 1947.
commanders: Lt. Kenneth Arthur Gadd, DSC, RNR (25 Aug 1943 - mid 1945)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Sydney Frederick Harmer-Elliot, RNVR (20 Dec 1944 - 29 May 1945)
Lt. Henry Russell Richards, RN (29 May 1945 - 1 Jul 1946)
Lt. Edward George Mason, RN (Aug 1944 - Aug 1946)

3814.built_by: Port Arthur Shipbuilding Co. (Port Arthur, Ontario, Canada)
3814.history: Decommissioned 3 August 1945. recommissioned in 1948 as hydrographic survey ship with penant number 170. Classified as an oceanographic survey ship from 1958. Sold for scrap in 1974.
commanders: T/Lt. Donald Egerton Ryerson, RCNVR (4 Oct 1944 - 3 Aug 1945)

3815.history: Returned to the United States Navy in January 1947.
commanders: A/Lt.Cdr. (retired) Edwin Tom Durrant, RNR (15 Sep 1943 - 13 Sep 1944)
Cdr. John Curthoys Richards, RN (13 Sep 1944 - 29 Nov 1944)
Cdr. Duncan Lachlan Johnston, RN (29 Nov 1944 - Feb 1946)

3816.history: Returned to the United States Navy on 10 December 1946. Sold to Greece in October 1948.
commanders: Cdr. (retired) Frederick Richard Guy Maunsell, RN (18 Sep 1943 - 13 Feb 1945)
Lt.Cdr. Thomas Tod Euman, RN (17 Feb 1945 - late 1945)

3817.built_by: Port Arthur Shipbuilding Co. (Port Arthur, Ontario, Canada)
commanders: T/Lt. Harold Johnson, RNR (21 Nov 1944 - 17 Mar 1945)
Lt.Cdr. Charles David Sampson, DSC, RNVR (17 Mar 1945 - 20 Jul 1946)

3818.history: Served in the East Indies and Pacific at the end of the war. Returned through the Mediterranean to Devonport (Plymouth) arriving there on 10 September 1946. Scrapped at Llanelli, Wales starting on 8 August 1960.
commanders: Lcmdr John Penri Davies, RNR (7 Oct 1944 - 2 Jul 1945)
A/Lt.Cdr. Peter Peterson, RNZNVR (2 Jul 1945 - 17 Apr 1946)

3819.history: At 1705 hours on 9 May 1945 HMS Prompt (T/A/Lt.Cdr. R.K. Hart, RANVR) was mined 12 nautical miles north-west off Ostend, Belgium. She was towed to Southend and declared a constructive total loss, not being worth repairing. HMS Prompt is not listed as active unit in the October 1945 Navy List Sold to be broken up for scrap on 16 January 1947.
commanders: Lt. Peter Peterson, RNZNVR (6 Dec 1943 - 15 Mar 1944)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Robert Kenley Hart, RANVR (15 Mar 1945 - Jul 1945)
T/Lt. Robert Kell, RNVR (Mar 1945 - 4 Oct 1945)

3821.built_by: Port Arthur Shipbuilding Co. (Port Arthur, Ontario, Canada)
3821.history: Decommissioned 27 March 1946. Recommissioned as oceanographic observation ship with penant number 171 in 1948. Decommissioned in 1972. Sunk as a target in 1978.
commanders: A/Lt.Cdr. Angus Hetherington Rankin, OBE, RCNVR (21 Jun 1944 - 2 Sep 1945)

3822.built_by: Port Arthur Shipbuilding Co. (Port Arthur, Ontario, Canada)
3822.history: Sold to the Burmese Navy on 18 April 1958.

commanders: Lt.Cdr. Trevor Montagu Williams, RNR (6 Jan 1944 - 24 Jun 1944)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Adam Steel Drysdale, RNVR (24 Jun 1944 - 1 Oct 1945)

3826.history: HMS Regulus (Cdr. Roger William David Thomson, DSC, RN) was mined off Corfu on 12 January 1945.

3834.built_by: Port Arthur Shipbuilding Co. (Port Arthur, Ontario, Canada)
3834.history: In November 1944 her commander became Senior Officer of Escort Group W3. Decommissioned 31 December 1945. Became a search and rescue ship in 1946. Wrecked off New Scotland on 3 December 1946.
commanders: T/Lt. Walter James Piercey, RCNR (20 Mar 1944 - 5 Jan 1946)
A/Lt.Cdr. (retired) Edwin Tom Durrant, RNR (27 Sep 1944 - Jan 1945)
Lt. John Bruce Lamb, DSC, RN (1 Jan 1945 - 19 Aug 1946)

3836.history: In July 1945 she joined the 10th MSF, Nore Command Nov 1945 10th MSF, East Indies Jan 1946 11th MSF Feb 1946 7th MSF June 1946 11th MSF Paid off in Feb 1947 to Category B reserve at Singapore. She was bought by the Royal Thai Navy in April 1947 and renamed HTMS PHOSAMTON on 20 Nov 1947 She attended the Queens Coronation Spithead review in 1953 whilst on Naval Cadet training. She is still in service as a training ship and is believed to be the oldest Steam Engined warship still in service. www.bigdude.freeserve.co.uk/minstrel.htm
commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. Edward Beaumont Cutlack, RNR (9 Jun 1945 - Oct 1945)
T/Lt. Thomas Norman Baker, RNR (Oct 1945 - ???)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. James Alexander Hill, DSC, RNVR (9 Jul 1945 - Dec 1945)
